etymology.description: 非常 (fēicháng, "very; extremely") combines two characters: 非(非) (fēi, "not; wrong") and 常(常) (cháng, "ordinary; usual"). 非(非) indicates negation or contrariety, while 常(常) refers to the usual or regular state of things.
etymology.interpretation: The combination 非(非) (fēi, "not; wrong") + 常(常) (cháng, "ordinary; usual") creates an image of something that is 'not ordinary' or 'out of the ordinary', leading to an intensified sense of something being beyond the norm or extremely so. This mechanism forms the basis for its use to mean 'very' or 'extremely', as it indicates a departure from the expected or typical state.

examples.chinese: 天气非常好(天氣非常好)。
今天的食物非常美味(今天的食物非常美味)。
他工作非常努力(他工作非常努力)，因此得到了晋升(因此得到了晉升)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gōngzuò fēicháng nǔlì, yīncǐ dédào le jìnshēng.
examples.english: He works very hard, so he got a promotion.
